input_dict.agent_role: Summarize first-hop evidence and expose the bridge entity, missing fact, or retrieval direction needed for the next hop.

task_context.question: What was the name of Red Hot Chili Peppers 1989 album released by EMI Records?
task_context.agent_role: Summarize first-hop evidence and expose the bridge entity, missing fact, or retrieval direction needed for the next hop.

left_state.output: Passage [3] states: "Sock-Cess is a 1989 compilation album by Red Hot Chili Peppers and was released exclusively in the UK by EMI as a promotional only release." Passage [2] confirms earlier EMI releases: the band's debut was "released on August 10, 1984 on EMI Records." Missing/next-hop: confirm there were no other 1989 RHCP albums issued by EMI (or verify "Sock-Cess" as the official 1989 EMI release) by checking Red Hot Chili Peppers discography or EMI release/catalog records for 1989 (use the quoted phrase "Sock-Cess is a 1989 compilation album" and "released exclusively in the UK by EMI" as anchors).
left_state.hop2_query: Sock-Cess "1989 compilation album" "released exclusively in the UK by EMI" Red Hot Chili Peppers discography
hop2_titles: The Best of Red Hot Chili Peppers
Under the Covers: Essential Red Hot Chili Peppers
Greatest Hits (Red Hot Chili Peppers album)
hop2_doc_snippets: Sock-Cess | Sock-Cess is a 1989 compilation album by Red Hot Chili Peppers and was released exclusively in the UK by EMI as a promotional only release.  The album contains tracks from the band's first four studio albums.

The Best of Red Hot Chili Peppers | The Best of Red Hot Chili Peppers (also titled Greatest Hits on some versions) is a compilation album by the Red Hot Chili Peppers, first released in 1994, containing tracks from the band's time signed to the EMI America record label. " What Hits!? " has all these songs and 8 more.  This compilation has been reprinted at least 4 times from 1994 to 2005 with different cover art.

The Abbey Road E.P. | The Abbey Road E.P. is an EP by The Red Hot Chili Peppers, released in the UK in May 1988 through EMI America as a way of introducing UK fans to the band's back catalog as they were touring the UK that year.  Out of the five tracks included on this EP, four had already been previously released on the band's studio albums, although the band's first two albums The Red Hot Chili Peppers and Freaky Styley were not released in the UK until 1990, thus it provided a way of introdu ...[truncated]

Under the Covers: Essential Red Hot Chili Peppers | Under the Covers: Essential Red Hot Chili Peppers is a compilation album released on March 31, 1998 by the Red Hot Chili Peppers.  All of the songs featured are covers mainly from the band's early albums.

The Red Hot Chili Peppers (album) | The Red Hot Chili Peppers is the debut studio album by American rock band Red Hot Chili Peppers, released on August 10, 1984 on EMI Records.  The album was produced by Gang of Four guitarist Andy Gill, and is the only album to feature Jack Sherman on guitar.  Sherman was fired by the band after the album release tour, replaced by founding member Hillel Slovak. " Get Up and Jump" was the only single released from the album, but a music video was made for the so ...[truncated]
Out in L.A. | Out in L.A. is a compilation of rare tracks by the Red Hot Chili Peppers released November 1, 1994 on EMI.

Greatest Hits (Red Hot Chili Peppers album) | Greatest Hits is the second "best-of" compilation album by the Red Hot Chili Peppers.  It was released on November 18, 2003 by Warner Bros.  Records.  Aside from their cover of "Higher Ground", all songs on the compilation are from their tenure on Warner Bros.  Records from 1991 to 2002, in addition to two newly recorded songs.
left_state.summary_2: The 1989 Red Hot Chili Peppers release on EMI was the compilation "Sock-Cess" — "Sock-Cess is a 1989 compilation album by Red Hot Chili Peppers and was released exclusively in the UK by EMI" (passage [1]). No other 1989 EMI RHCP releases are shown in the supplied passages.

right_state.output: Summary of first-hop evidence: Passage for 'Sock-Cess' reports: "Sock-Cess is a 1989 compilation album by Red Hot Chili Peppers and was released exclusively in the UK by EMI as a promotional only release." The band's self-titled debut page confirms earlier EMI association: the debut album was "released on August 10, 1984 on EMI Records." Bridge / missing fact needed next: confirm which 1989 Red Hot Chili Peppers release (if any besides the cited compilation) was issued by EMI, and verify the official 1989 EMI release information for 'Sock-Cess' (e.g., whether it is the only 1989 EMI-issued RHCP album). Compact retrieval direction for next hop: 1) Open the 'Mother's Milk' page to check its release date and record label (to see if it was a 1989 EMI release). 2) Open the 'Good Time Boys' page (and any single/compilation pages) to check label/year. 3) Consult the Red Hot Chili Peppers discography or EMI catalog entries for 1989 to confirm whether any other RHCP album was released by EMI in 1989 and to corroborate the quoted phrases. Use these anchor queries/phrases when retrieving: "Sock-Cess is a 1989 compilation album" and "released exclusively in the UK by EMI as a promotional only release", plus "Mother's Milk release date" and "Mother's Milk record label." This is a summary for the next-hop retriever, not a final answer.

This is the band's first studio album since 2011's "I'm with You".  It was produced by Danger Mouse, who replaced Rick Rubin after twenty-five years and six albums as the band's producer, making it the first non-Rubin produced album since 1989's "Mother's Milk", while its release makes the current lineup of the band onl ...[truncated]
right_state.summary_2: Sock-Cess is a 1989 compilation album by Red Hot Chili Peppers released exclusively in the UK by EMI as a promotional-only release. The band's fourth studio album, Mother's Milk, was also released by EMI on August 16, 1989.

request.rendered_prompt:
You are working with a functional semantic distance over states of one
agent in a multi-agent pipeline.

Each state contains the selected agent's output and may also contain the
downstream consequences produced by that output while the rest of the
pipeline is held fixed. The agent output is the primary object of the
transition. Downstream evidence and metrics are supporting evidence about
its functional effect.

The distance is not surface text edit distance and is not a correctness
score. It measures how much task-relevant behavior and information must
change to move from one state to another.

Judge distance using:
1. Agent-output behavior: what the output is trying to accomplish for the
   selected agent's role.
2. Task-relevant information: which entities, relations, evidence,
   conclusions, or answer constraints are preserved, added, removed, or
   corrected.
3. Downstream consequence: how the output changes retrieval, evidence,
   answerability, or information available to later agents.
4. Transition utility: whether a state is a meaningful intermediate step
   between two endpoint states.

Do not infer distance from text length, token count, document count, or
lexical overlap. Do not treat a state as closer merely because its metric
or final answer is better. Metrics are evidence of functional consequence,
not the distance definition itself.

A midpoint output o_mid between o_left and o_right must:
- preserve useful task-relevant behavior or information from o_left;
- make concrete semantic progress toward o_right;
- differ meaningfully from both endpoint outputs;
- not merely paraphrase or copy o_left;
- not simply copy o_right;
- represent two smaller functional transitions than the original edge.

Compare all adjacent transitions jointly and select the single edge with
the largest functional semantic distance.

Use the same distance definition for every edge. If two edges are
functionally tied, select the lower edge_index.

Agent:
summary1
